articles.title: Hack of the day: Translate entire websites in Chrome
articles.description: Navigating foreign language websites is now effortless thanks to Chrome's integrated translation tool. This feature allows instant, full-page conversions, eliminating the need for manual copying and pasting. From students accessing global research to travellers planning trips, this browser utility unlocks a world of information, making international content readily accessible for everyone.

articles.title: ASUS ROG Expands Gaming Display Portfolio With New Strix OLED Ultrawide Monitor and Secondary Touch Display
articles.description: ASUS ROG has expanded its premium gaming monitor lineup with the launch of two new display solutions aimed at gamers, streamers, and advanced PC users. The company introduced the new ROG Strix OLED XG34WCDMS ultrawide gaming monitor alongside the compact ROG Strix XG129C secondary display. The latest additions focus on... The post ASUS ROG Expands Gaming Display Portfolio With New Strix OLED Ultrawide Monitor and Secondary Touch Display appeared first on DEVICENEXT .

articles.title: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink disagrees with everyone who says there is AI bubble; says: It's actually the opposite, as we have severe shortage of ...
articles.description: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink refutes AI bubble concerns, citing a significant shortage in computing capacity, chips, and memory. He predicts a future where financial markets will trade futures of compute, highlighting the growing demand for AI infrastructure. BlackRock is actively investing in data centers and energy assets to meet this demand.

articles.title: iOS 26.5 to roll out soon: Supported iPhones, and new features it will bring
articles.description: Apple is expected to roll out the stable version of iOS 26.5 this week after recently releasing the Release Candidate (RC) build to developers and public beta testers. While the company has not officially confirmed the rollout date yet, reports suggest that the update could begin arriving for eligible iPhone users soon.

articles.title: OpenAI let employees cash out up to $30 million each in share sale last year: Report
articles.description: OpenAI employees, both current and former, collectively sold $6.6 billion in shares during a secondary transaction in October 2025. This sale valued the artificial intelligence company at an estimated $400 billion, allowing approximately 75 participants to liquidate up to $30 million each in equity.

articles.description: India's space commercial arm, NSIL, is poised for a significant shift as a private sector leader, reportedly from Boeing, is the front-runner for its CMD post. This marks a departure from its history of being led by Isro insiders. The selection process is in its final stages, aiming to bring external expertise to the burgeoning space PSU.

articles.title: Snapseed just got its biggest Android update in two years: Here's everything new
articles.description: Google rolled out Snapseed 4.0 for Android on May 8, ending a two-year update drought. The release brings a built-in Snapseed Camera with real-time film emulations inspired by Kodak, Fujifilm, and Polaroid stocks, plus batch editing, non-destructive editing, and new tools like Halation, Bloom, and Color HSL. iOS got a head start with version 3.0 last summer—Android skipped straight to 4.0 to match it. Everything is still free.

articles.title: Curb your overenthusiasm: Why India must avoid early overregulation while drafting rules for new tech
articles.description: India faces a critical choice in regulating new industries like AI and genomics. Overly cautious, ex-ante regulations, similar to the historical Red Flag Act, can stifle innovation and lead to economic losses. The country must adopt flexible approaches like regulatory sandboxes and impact assessments. This will determine if India builds these future industries or exports them elsewhere.

articles.title: DOGE Used ChatGPT to Cut $100m of Humanities Funding, Says Judge
articles.description: DOGE officials instructed OpenAI’s chatbot to analyze spreadsheets filled with short descriptions of humanities grants, according to the filing.

While this likely isn’t a huge problem quite yet, I do think it raises some questions going forward. more...

articles.title: "Dropping like butterflies": Trump's Truth Social feed blurs lines with AI war imagery, LIV Golf highlights
articles.description: Former President Donald Trump posted over a dozen AI-generated images on Truth Social depicting maritime warfare between the US and Iran, alongside golf event snapshots. The fabricated visuals, shared after a period of silence, targeted political rivals and aimed to validate past claims of military success against Tehran.

articles.description: Russian private space company Space Energy plans to begin construction of its own spaceport in Primorsky Territory in 2026 for launching ultra-light rockets, the company told Sputnik.

articles.title: Why Sony just brought TSMC into its most important business
articles.description: Sony and TSMC are joining forces in Japan to develop cutting-edge image sensors, with Sony retaining majority control. This strategic partnership, leveraging Sony's design expertise and TSMC's manufacturing might, aims to dominate future markets like AI-driven cars and robotics. The move signals Sony's shift towards a 'fab-light' model, focusing on intellectual property while facing increasing competition.

This year's exams, conducted from February 17 to April 10, benefited from a new On-Screen Marking system for faster, more accurate evaluations.
